steps.title: Find H's coordinates and compute angle FGH
steps.content:
Since H = (−1, y) lies on the unit circle, we apply the circle equation:
$$(−1)^2 + y^2 = 1$$
$$y^2 = 0 \implies y = 0$$
So H = (−1, 0). This makes F = (1, 0) and H = (−1, 0) diametrically opposite points on the circle. The angle at center G between them is:
$$\angle FGH = \pi \text{ radians}$$

steps.title: Find all possible positive angle measures
steps.content:
Since angles can be measured by adding full rotations (multiples of 2π), all positive measures of angle FGH have the form:
$$\pi + 2\pi k = (2k+1)\pi \quad \text{for } k = 0, 1, 2, \ldots$$
This gives the sequence: π, 3π, 5π, 7π, 9π, 11π, 13π,... — all odd multiples of π.

steps.title: Identify which choice is an odd multiple of π
steps.content:
• A) $\frac{13\pi}{2} = 6.5\pi$ — not an integer multiple of π
  • B) $\frac{15\pi}{2} = 7.5\pi$ — not an integer multiple of π
  • C) $10\pi$ — even multiple of π (10 = 2×5)
  • D) $11\pi$ — odd multiple of π, since $11 = 2(5) + 1$ ✓
That matches choice D — D is correct.
